---
title: "data modeling by Yaxing Li"
author: "Yaxing Li"
date: "2025-03-07"
output: html_document
---

# Modeling

## 1.Preparation of data and packages
```{R}
library("tidyverse")
library("tidymodels")
library(shapviz)  # Needs development version 0.9.0 from github
sessionInfo()
data <- read_csv("final_engineered_data.csv")
```

## 2.transforming the label variable
```{R}
data |>  dim()
data |> head()
# Split the data into training set
train_data <- data %>% 
  filter(split == "train") %>% 
  mutate(label=factor(label, levels=c('baseline','meditation','stress','break')))  # Convert the label column to factor

test_data <- data %>% 
  filter(split == "test")

```

## 3. check participants intersect
```{R}
train_subjects <- train_data%>% select(participant_id) %>% distinct()
test_subjects <- test_data %>% select(participant_id) %>% distinct()
intersect(train_subjects, test_subjects)
```

## 4.Train-Test split
```{r}
set.seed(0) 
# Remove 'split' from the dataset before creating the recipe
train_data_clean <- train_data %>% select(-split)
# Split the data
data_split <- group_initial_split(train_data_clean, prop = 5/6, group = participant_id) 
train_split_data <- training(data_split)
val_split_data <- testing(data_split)
```

## 5. Create the workflow
```{R}
# Load necessary libraries
library(tidymodels)

# Define a preprocessing recipe
train_recipe <- recipe(label ~ ., data = train_split_data) %>%
  update_role(chunk_id, new_role = "ID") %>%  
  update_role(participant_id, new_role = "ID") %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%  # Normalize numerical features
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%      # One-hot encode categorical variables
  step_zv(all_predictors())                     # Remove zero-variance predictors

# Prepare the recipe
prepped_recipe <- prep(train_recipe, training = train_split_data)

# Apply transformations
train_processed <- bake(prepped_recipe, new_data = train_split_data)
val_processed <- bake(prepped_recipe, new_data = val_split_data)

```

## 6. random forest model training
```{R}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ranger)  # High-performance Random Forest implementation

# Define a Random Forest model
rf_model <- rand_forest(mode = "classification", trees = 500) %>%  # 500 trees
  set_engine("ranger", importance = "impurity")  # Enables feature importance calculation

# Create a workflow
train_workflow <- workflow() %>%
  add_model(rf_model) %>%
  add_recipe(train_recipe)

# Train the Random Forest model
trained_model <- fit(train_workflow, data = train_processed)

# Make predictions on validation data
val_predictions <- predict(trained_model, val_processed, type = "class") %>%
  bind_cols(val_split_data)  # Combine predictions with actual validation data

# Evaluate model performance
val_metrics <- val_predictions %>%
  metrics(truth = label, estimate = .pred_class)

print(val_metrics)
```

## hypoerparameter tuning
```{R}
# Define Random Forest model with tuning parameters
rf_model_tune <- rand_forest(
  mode = "classification",
  trees = tune(),   # Tune number of trees
  mtry = tune(),    # Tune number of features per split
  min_n = tune()    # Tune minimum node size
) %>%
  set_engine("ranger", importance = "impurity")

# Define cross-validation folds
set.seed(123)
cv_folds <- vfold_cv(train_split_data, v = 5)

# Define a grid of hyperparameters to search
rf_grid <- grid_random(
  trees(range = c(100, 1000)),  # Tune trees from 100 to 1000
  mtry(range = c(2, ncol(train_split_data) - 2)),  # Tune feature subset
  min_n(range = c(2, 20)),  # Tune minimum samples per node
  size = 10  # Search across 10 random combinations
)

# Define workflow
rf_workflow_tune <- workflow() %>%
  add_model(rf_model_tune) %>%
  add_recipe(train_recipe)

# Run hyperparameter tuning with cross-validation
set.seed(123)
rf_tuned_results <- tune_grid(
  rf_workflow_tune,
  resamples = cv_folds,
  grid = rf_grid,
  metrics = metric_set(accuracy, kap)  # Evaluate on accuracy & Kappa
)

# Select best model based on accuracy
best_params <- select_best(rf_tuned_results, metric = "accuracy")

# Finalize the workflow with the best parameters
final_rf_model <- finalize_model(rf_model_tune, best_params)
final_rf_workflow <- rf_workflow_tune %>%
  update_model(final_rf_model)

# Train the final model on the full training set
final_trained_model <- fit(final_rf_workflow, data = train_split_data)

# Make predictions on validation data
val_predictions <- predict(final_trained_model, val_split_data) %>%
  bind_cols(val_split_data)

# Evaluate model performance
val_metrics <- val_predictions %>%
  metrics(truth = label, estimate = .pred_class)

print(val_metrics)  # Check final accuracy & Kappa

final_model <- fit(final_trained_model, data = train_data_clean)
# Step 10: Make predictions on the test data
submission_data <- 
  augment(final_model, test_data, type = "class") %>% 
  select(participant_id, chunk_id, .pred_class)

# Save the predictions
submission_data %>%
  rename(label = .pred_class) %>%
  write.csv('DSW25_rf_model_1.csv', row.names = FALSE)
```

## feature importance
```{R}
library(vip)

# Extract feature importance from the trained model
feature_importance <- final_trained_model %>%
  extract_fit_parsnip() %>%  # Extract fitted ranger model
  pluck("fit") %>%  # Access the ranger model
  importance() %>%  # Get feature importance scores
  enframe(name = "Feature", value = "Importance") %>%
  arrange(desc(Importance))  # Sort from highest to lowest

# Print feature importance table
print(feature_importance)

# Plot feature importance (Top 15 features)
feature_importance %>%
  top_n(15, Importance) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Feature, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +  # Flip for better visualization
  labs(title = "Feature Importance (Top 30)", x = "Feature", y = "Importance") +
  theme_minimal()

```


## feature selection
```{R}
# Define a threshold for feature importance
importance_threshold <- 2

# Filter out features with importance below the threshold
important_features <- feature_importance %>%
  filter(Importance >= importance_threshold) %>%
  pull(Feature)  # Get the names of the important features

# Define a preprocessing recipe with only important features
train_recipe_filtered <- recipe(label ~ ., data = train_split_data) %>%
  update_role(chunk_id, new_role = "ID") %>%  
  update_role(participant_id, new_role = "ID") %>%
  # Keep the outcome variable (label) intact
  update_role(label, new_role = "outcome") %>%
  # Select only important features while keeping the outcome column intact
  update_role(all_of(important_features), new_role = "predictor") %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%  # Normalize numerical features
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%      # One-hot encode categorical variables
  step_zv(all_predictors())                     # Remove zero-variance predictors

# Define the Random Forest model with hyperparameters to tune
rf_model <- rand_forest(
  mode = "classification",
  trees = tune(),   # Tune the number of trees
  mtry = tune(),    # Tune the mtry (number of features to sample)
  min_n = tune()    # Tune the minimum node size
) %>%
  set_engine("ranger", importance = "impurity")

# Define the workflow
rf_workflow <- workflow() %>%
  add_model(rf_model) %>%
  add_recipe(train_recipe_filtered)

# Define the parameter grid for tuning (expanded ranges)
rf_grid <- grid_regular(
  trees(range = c(200, 300)),  # Expanded range for trees
  mtry(range = c(2, length(important_features))),  # Grid search for mtry
  min_n(range = c(1, 20)),  # Expanded range for min_n
  levels = 5  # Number of levels for each hyperparameter
)

# Perform grid search to find the best hyperparameters
tune_results <- tune_grid(
  rf_workflow,
  resamples = vfold_cv(train_split_data, v = 10),  # 10-fold cross-validation
  grid = rf_grid,
  metrics = metric_set(accuracy, kap)  # Additional metrics
)

# View the best parameters from tuning
best_params <- tune_results %>%
  select_best(metric = "accuracy")  # Select best parameters based on accuracy

print(best_params)

# Re-train the model with the best hyperparameters
rf_model_final <- rand_forest(
  mode = "classification",
  trees = best_params$trees,
  mtry = best_params$mtry,
  min_n = best_params$min_n
) %>%
  set_engine("ranger", importance = "impurity")

# Update the workflow with the final model
rf_workflow_final_feature_selected <- workflow() %>%
  add_model(rf_model_final) %>%
  add_recipe(train_recipe_filtered)

# Train the model with the best parameters
trained_model_final <- fit(rf_workflow_final_feature_selected, data = train_split_data)

# Make predictions on validation data
val_predictions_filtered <- predict(trained_model_final, val_split_data) %>%
  bind_cols(val_split_data)

# Evaluate the performance of the final model using multiple metrics
val_metrics_filtered <- val_predictions_filtered %>%
  metrics(truth = label, estimate = .pred_class) %>%
  bind_cols()

print(val_metrics_filtered)

final_model <- fit(trained_model_final, data = train_data_clean)
# Step 10: Make predictions on the test data
submission_data <- 
  augment(final_model, test_data, type = "class") %>% 
  select(participant_id, chunk_id, .pred_class)

# Save the predictions
submission_data %>%
  rename(label = .pred_class) %>%
  write.csv('DSW25_rf_model_2_feature_selection.csv', row.names = FALSE)
```

## fit model to the test data
```{R}
final_model <- fit(train_workflow, train_data)  # use all the data available for your model
submission_data <- 
  augment(final_model, test_data, type='class') %>% 
  select(participant_id, chunk_id, .pred_class)
submission_data %>%
  rename(label = .pred_class)%>%
  select(participant_id,chunk_id, label) %>%
  write.csv('DSW25_knn_11_model_1.csv')
```

## hypoerparameter tuning
```{R}
# Load the randomForest package
library(randomForest)

set.seed(0)

# Define the control method for RFE
ctrl <- rfeControl(functions=rfFuncs, method="cv", number=10)  # 10-fold cross-validation

# Perform RFE for feature selection using KNN as the model
rfe_results <- rfe(train_processed[, -which(names(train_processed) == "label")], 
                   train_processed$label, 
                   sizes = c(1:5, 10, 15, 20),   # Sizes of features to try
                   rfeControl = ctrl)

# View the results of RFE
print(rfe_results)

# Extract the feature importance from the RFE results
importance_data <- as.data.frame(rfe_results$variables)
importance_data$Variable <- rownames(importance_data)

# Sort the features by importance (descending order)
importance_data_sorted <- importance_data[order(importance_data$Overall, decreasing = TRUE), ]

# Select the most and least important features
top_features <- head(importance_data_sorted, 20)  # Top 10 most important features
bottom_features <- tail(importance_data_sorted, 20)  # Bottom 10 least important features

# Combine the top and bottom features for plotting
top_bottom_features <- rbind(top_features, bottom_features)

# Plot the most and least important features separately
ggplot(top_bottom_features, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Overall), y = Overall)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "skyblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Most and Least Important Features (Based on RFE)",
       x = "Features",
       y = "Importance") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

## retain the model with out negative features
```{R}
# It seems mean_bvp and skewness_temp has the most negative impact on the model so I took them out and retain the model.
set.seed(0)
# Remove 'split' from the dataset before creating the recipe
train_data_clean <- train_data %>% 
  select(-split, -skewness_temp, -mean_bvp)  # Remove least important features

# Split the data
data_split <- group_initial_split(train_data_clean, prop = 5/6, group = participant_id) 
train_split_data <- training(data_split)
val_split_data <- testing(data_split)

# Step 1: Set up Cross-Validation
cv_folds <- vfold_cv(train_split_data, v = 5)  # 5-fold cross-validation

# Step 2: Define the KNN Model with tune() for 'neighbors'
knn_model <- nearest_neighbor(mode = "classification", neighbors = tune()) %>%
  set_engine("kknn")

# Step 3: Define a grid of values for the 'neighbors' hyperparameter
neighbors_grid <- grid_regular(neighbors(range = c(1, 20)), levels = 20)

# Step 4: Define a recipe (same as before, if necessary)
train_recipe <- recipe(label ~ ., data = train_split_data) %>%
  update_role(chunk_id, new_role = "ID") %>%  
  update_role(participant_id, new_role = "ID") %>% 
  step_impute_knn(all_predictors(), neighbors = 4) %>%   # KNN imputation
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%  
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%      
  step_zv(all_predictors())

# Step 5: Create a Workflow
tune_workflow <- workflow() %>%
  add_model(knn_model) %>%
  add_recipe(train_recipe)

# Step 6: Tune the model with cross-validation
tune_results <- tune_grid(
  tune_workflow,
  resamples = cv_folds,  # Cross-validation folds
  grid = neighbors_grid,
  control = control_grid(save_pred = TRUE)
)

# Step 7: Get the best model
best_k <- select_best(tune_results, metric = "accuracy")  # Choose based on accuracy
best_k

# Step 8: Finalize the workflow with the best 'k'= 9
final_knn_model <- nearest_neighbor(mode = "classification", neighbors = best_k$neighbors) %>%
  set_engine("kknn")

# Step 9: Train the final model with the optimal 'k'
final_model <- fit(final_knn_model, label ~ ., data = train_split_data)

# Step 10: Evaluate on validation data
val_predictions <- predict(final_model, val_split_data) %>%
  bind_cols(val_split_data)

val_metrics <- val_predictions %>%
  metrics(truth = label, estimate = .pred_class)

print(val_metrics)

```

## fit model with out non_important features to the test data
```{R}
# Step 9: Train the final model on the entire training set
final_model <- fit(final_knn_model, label ~ ., data = train_data_clean)
# Step 10: Make predictions on the test data
submission_data <- 
  augment(final_model, test_data, type = "class") %>% 
  select(participant_id, chunk_id, .pred_class)

# Save the predictions
submission_data %>%
  rename(label = .pred_class) %>%
  write.csv('DSW25_knn_11_model_without_2_features_2.csv', row.names = FALSE)
```
